steps.text: Identify the sample mean and margin of error: the estimated average is $23.1$ pounds with a margin of error of $0.19$ pounds.
Compute the confidence interval bounds: lower bound $= 23.1 - 0.19 = 22.91$ pounds; upper bound $= 23.1 + 0.19 = 23.29$ pounds.
Interpret the margin of error: plausible values for the true population average lie within the interval $[22.91,\ 23.29]$ pounds. Values outside this range are not well supported by the data.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: Plausible values are between $22.91$ and $23.29$ pounds — this correctly describes the confidence interval. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: Plausible values are less than $22.91$ or greater than $23.29$ — this describes the region outside the confidence interval, which is the opposite of what a margin of error implies. Eliminated.
  • C: The average is less than $23.01$ pounds — $23.01 = 23.1 - 0.09$ is not one of the bounds and the statement ignores the upper range. Eliminated.
  • D: The average is greater than $23.01$ pounds — same issue; this truncates the interval incorrectly. Eliminated.
